div1.entryFree: Cinque-pace (O. Edd. Cinque-pace and Sink-apace), a dance, 'the steps of which were regulated by the number five' (Nares): wooing, wedding, and repenting, is as a Scotch jig, a measure, and a c.; . . . and then comes repentance and, with his bad legs, falls into the c. faster and faster, till he sink into his grave, Ado II, 1, 77. I would not so much as make water but in a c. Tw. I, 3, 139.
